perturbation.original_text: 4.1 The Executive shall serve as Chief Medical Officer and Senior Vice President, Development (reporting to Rick Winningham) or in such other role as the Company may determine.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 The Executive shall serve as Chief Medical Officer and Senior Vice President, Development (reporting to Rick Winningham). The company guarantees this role will remain unchanged for the duration of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause "The company guarantees this role will remain unchanged for the duration of this agreement" we contradict the original section 4.1 where it says "or in such other role as the Company may determine." which introduces uncertainty about the Executive's role and the company's ability to alter it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 The Executive shall serve as Chief Medical Officer and Senior Vice President, Development (reporting to Rick Winningham). The company guarantees this role will remain unchanged for the duration of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a guarantee that the executive's role will remain unchanged, directly contradicting the original clause in the same section that allows the company to determine a different role for the executive. This introduces a clear and direct conflict regarding the company's ability to alter the executive's role, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability. The contradictory language appears in section 4.1.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 The Executive shall be entitled to 28 days' paid holiday in each holiday year (which includes public holidays) to be taken at times convenient to the Company and authorised in advance.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 The Executive shall be entitled to 28 days' paid holiday in each holiday year (which includes public holidays) to be taken at times convenient to the Executive and authorized by them without company interference.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the clause from "taken at times convenient to the Company and authorised in advance" to "taken at times convenient to the Executive and authorized by them without company interference" creates a contradiction. Originally the company had control on when the Executive could take holidays, but now the Executive has full control and there is uncertainty about which party dictates when holidays are taken. The change introduces an in-text contradiction in section 12.1 by directly conflicting which entity has the ability to dictate the schedule.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 The Executive shall be entitled to 28 days' paid holiday in each holiday year (which includes public holidays) to be taken at times convenient to the Executive and authorized by them without company interference.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the control over holiday scheduling from the Company to the Executive. This directly contradicts the original text's intention and creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding who has the authority to dictate holiday schedules. The Contradicted Text is a direct copy of the Changed Text in 12.1 which leads to the conclusion that there is a contracdiction in the same place. The contradiction is strong because the initial power dynamic of the company dictating the schedule is completely switched so the executive dictates it instead.

perturbation.original_text: 17.1 The Company may, in its sole and absolute discretion, terminate the Executive's employment under this Agreement at any time and with immediate effect by notifying the Executive that the Company is exercising its right under this clause 17 and that it will make a payment in l ieu of not ice ("PILON") to the Executive. The Executive's employment will terminate immediately and any PILON due to the Executive in accordance with the 8 provisions of this clause 17 will be paid within 28 days. The PILON will be equal to the basic salary (as at the Termination Date) which the Executive would have been entitled to receive under this Agreement during the notice period referred to at clause 2 (or, if notice has already been given, during the remainder of the notice period) less deductions required by law.
perturbation.changed_text: 17.1 The Company may, in its sole and absolute discretion, terminate the Executive's employment under this Agreement at any time. Should the company choose to terminate without cause or the agreement of the Executive, the company is expected to provide a payment in l ieu of not ice ("PILON") to the Executive. The Executive's employment will terminate after 90 days notice, and any PILON due to the Executive in accordance with the provisions of this clause 17 will be paid within 28 days. The PILON will be equal to two times the basic salary (as at the Termination Date) which the Executive would have been entitled to receive under this Agreement during the notice period referred to at clause 2 (or, if notice has already been given, during the remainder of the notice period) less deductions required by law.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces several contradictions. The original clause allowed for immediate termination, while the modified version requires 90 days notice. Also, the original clause has PILON, whereas the modified version dictates that PILON is only given if the company is terminating with cause and the amount is different (2x the salary). By making these changes we are creating uncertainty in section 17.1 since we contradict the rules the company needs to follow when terminating the Executive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17.1 The Company may, in its sole and absolute discretion, terminate the Executive's employment under this Agreement at any time. Should the company choose to terminate without cause or the agreement of the Executive, the company is expected to provide a payment in l ieu of not ice ("PILON") to the Executive. The Executive's employment will terminate after 90 days notice, and any PILON due to the Executive in accordance with the provisions of this clause 17 will be paid within 28 days. The PILON will be equal to two times the basic salary (as at the Termination Date) which the Executive would have been entitled to receive under this Agreement during the notice period referred to at clause 2 (or, if notice has already been given, during the remainder of the notice period) less deductions required by law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple contradictions to the original agreement. First, it changes the termination clause from 'at any time and with immediate effect' to requiring '90 days notice'. This directly contradicts the original allowance for immediate termination. Second, it changes the conditions for PILON, stating it is only 'expected' when the company terminates 'without cause', whereas the original stated it was in lieu of notice with immediate effect. It also changes the amount due to two times the basic salary. These contradictions create uncertainty regarding the termination process, notice period, and compensation obligations, directly impacting enforceability and creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
